title: NOC 72022 – Supervisors, printing and related occupations | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Supervisors of Printing and Related Occupations (NOC 72022) are the operational leaders managing the transition from traditional offset printing to high-speed digital and 3D printing solutions. As the industry pivots toward sustainable packaging and on-demand publishing, these supervisors oversee teams of press operators, binders, and prepress technicians. Operating at the TEER 2 level, they combine technical mastery of color reproduction and film processing with the project management skills needed to meet 2026's rapid turnaround times and high-precision quality standards.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group720 – Technical trades contractors and supervisors

duties: Production Coordination: Supervising, coordinating, and scheduling the activities of workers who produce camera work, plates, cylinders, and finished printed products.
Quality Assurance: Reviewing and approving all job proofs or samples to ensure the quality of work meets exact client specifications and 2026 color-fidelity standards.
Technical Advice: Solving work-related problems, providing technical guidance, and recommending measures to improve productivity and product quality.
Resource Logistics: Ordering materials and supplies and ensuring printing presses and finishing equipment are maintained to preventive standards.
Training & HR: Training staff in job duties, safety procedures, and company policies, and recommending personnel actions like hiring and promotions.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship/College: Completion of a college program in Graphic Arts or a related field, or completion of an apprenticeship in a printing trade is usually necessary.
Experience: Several years of experience as a qualified Journeyperson in a printing trade (e.g., Press Operator or Prepress Technician) are mandatory.
Digital Skills: Proficiency in Print Management Information Systems (MIS) and high-end digital prepress software (e.g., Adobe Creative Suite, PitStop Pro).
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate, with job openings primarily driven by the need to replace a retiring workforce in the commercial printing and publishing sectors:

Ontario (GTA & Central Ontario): Moderate to Good. On March 25, 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72022 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker and International Student streams. Minimum scores for the Central Ontario region (excluding GTA) started at 55, signaling a specific regional need for printing leadership.
British Columbia: Moderate prospects. Demand is steady for high-end commercial printing and specialized packaging in the Lower Mainland. The median wage remains competitive at $34.95/hr.
Alberta: Stable demand. Focused on industrial printing and business forms for the energy and corporate sectors. Approximately 95% of workers in this NOC are employed full-time.
National Trend: While overall employment in traditional print is declining, the demand for Digital Print Supervisors and those managing "Smart Packaging" production lines is growing.
Why Work in Canada as a Printing Supervisor?
Lead the Digital Transformation: Direct the integration of AI-driven color management and automated finishing systems that define 2026 print shops.
Excellent Stability: Over 95% of supervisors in this NOC work full-time, which is significantly higher than the national average for other industrial occupations.
Cross-Sector Opportunities: Work for commercial printers, newspaper/magazine publishers, or in-house printing departments for major corporations and government agencies.
Priority for PR: In 2026, this NOC has become a regular feature in OINP regional draws, providing a structured pathway to Permanent Residency for those with a valid job offer.
pathways: Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): On March 25, 2026, OINP invited candidates in NOC 72022 with a score of 55+ (Central Ontario region) under the Foreign Worker stream. This is currently the most viable pathway for this role.
Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker): As a TEER 2 occupation, it qualifies for all general Express Entry draws. A provincial nomination can boost your score by 600 points.

2026 TR to PR Pathway: A federal initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Printing supervisors with established Canadian work experience are eligible under the "Essential Manufacturing and Services" categories.
Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot: A streamlined pathway for technical supervisors to obtain PR with a provincial job offer in the manufacturing or publishing sectors.
